page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.7 GHz, this ARMv9-based system features 768 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server includes 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the m8gd.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU physics tests. Its DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, which correlates with top-tier performance in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations, reaching 391.52 million operations per second. Single-core performance and cryptographic operations sit in the average tier, while LLM inference shows a distinct tradeoff: prompt processing is strong, but text generation speed falls into the bottom 25%.

Given its high resource density, dedicated bare-metal cores, and massive integrated NVMe storage, this server offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-throughput local storage and massive parallel processing without virtualization overhead. It is highly suited for large-scale database deployments, high-concurrency web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores powered by the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 768 GB of DDR5 memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, this instance delivers a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core alongside 50 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database execution, including Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance remains average, and LLM text generation speed is weak despite strong prompt processing capabilities. This server is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, large-scale software compilation, multi-threaded data processing, and high-concurrency static web serving where bare-metal access and massive local storage are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 ARM64 Graviton cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11,400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal ARM64 server featuring dedicated cores, massive local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core processing performance.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz
768 GB of high-speed DDR5 system memory and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
11,400 GB of high-performance local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and high-concurrency web serving
